question_text: On the Moon, the gravitational field strength is 1.6 N/kg compared to 10 N/kg on Earth. A person of mass 60 kg experiences different weights in each location. Calculate the difference in their weight between Earth and the Moon.
explanation: On Earth: Weight = 60 × 10 = 600 N. On Moon: Weight = 60 × 1.6 = 96 N. Difference = 600 - 96 = 504 N. The person weighs much less on the Moon because gravity is weaker there.
